description: Multi-round confidential request escalating sensitivity policy. Combines cross_session_guard (detect multi-round credential probing), rate_limit (throttle repeated sensitive queries), and budget_guard (hard cap on sensitive interactions per session). When a user repeatedly requests confidential information across rounds, the system escalates from warning to throttling to hard deny.

rules.description: Detect multi-round credential probing: if the same session requests access to 2+ distinct credential categories (passwords, API keys, tokens, certificates), flag as potential exfiltration chain.
Throttle users who issue more than 5 sensitive-topic queries within a 60-second window. This provides a graduated response before hard denial.
rules.revise_template: Your request frequency for sensitive topics has been throttled. Please wait before retrying.

rules.description: Hard cap: no more than 10 sensitive-topic interactions per session. After this budget is exhausted, all subsequent sensitive requests are denied outright.
Flag inputs that exhibit escalation rhetoric (e.g., 'I already have access', 'just need one more', 'my manager approved') which are common in social engineering attacks.
rules.revise_template: Authorization claims in prompts cannot be verified. Please use proper authorization channels.

metadata.author: security-kernel-team
metadata.use_case: Protecting systems where users may attempt multi-round credential extraction through gradual escalation. Combines three defense layers: capability dependency detection (cross_session_guard), rate limiting, and hard budget caps, demonstrating DSL composability.
metadata.deployment_notes: Enable all four rules simultaneously. The cross_session_guard provides semantic-level protection, while rate_limit and budget_guard provide quantitative bounds. The keyword_flag catches common social engineering patterns.
